titleStmt.title: Letter from M. Bonham Carter to Lord Wimborne, 19 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Maurice Bonham Carter

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter form Maurice Bonham Carter (1880-1960) to Ivor Churchill Guest (1873-1939), the Lord Wimborne. Carter writes on the behalf of King George V (1865-1936), accepting the resignation of Anthony Ashley-Cooper (1869-1961) as Lord Lieutenant of County Antrim. Ivor Churchill Guest, 1st Viscount Wimborne was appointed the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland in 1915. Guest worked closely alongside Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) in the administration of Ireland. Following the suppression of the Rising, Guest, under pressure, was forced to resign as Lord Lieutenant. A later inquiry into the Rising exonerated Guest and held Augustine Birrell (1850-1933) chiefly responsible for the events that occurred. Guest would continue to serve as Lord Lieutenant until 1918. Maurice Bonham Carter served as private secretary to British Prime Minister Herbert Asquith (1852-1928) and was a leading figure in the liberal government.

correspAction.placeName: 10 Downing Street, Whitehall, London S.W., England

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
10 Downing Street, Whitehall S.W. 19th February, 1916 My Lord The King having been pleased to accept the resignation
of the Earl of Shaftesbury from the Lieutenancy of the County
of Antrim, I have to request that the necessary steps may be
taken to give effect to His Majesty's commands. I have the honour to be,
Your Lordship's obedient Servant, M Bonham Carter His Excellency the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland. Rep.
Please annex papers
as to the appointment of Lord
Shaftesbury as H.M.L Co. Antrim & let the Sec
papers as to his resignation of the
Lieutenancy of the
City of Belfast
E. Aof.
24âIIâ16
